medications: Acetaminophen 500 mg as needed for knee pain (avoids NSAIDs due to CKD)
Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Ongoing anxiety and hypervigilance about being outed within her church and extended family; occasional low mood and sleep disruption, especially after tense family gatherings or rumors at work.
recent_visits: Nephrology follow-up at NYU Langone (eGFR monitoring, dietary counseling)
Primary care annual physical with blood pressure and lipid labs
Orthopedics visit for knee pain and physical therapy referral
Optometrist visit for updated prescription (dry eyes from cabin air)

finance.debt: Small remaining mortgage on a co-op in Queens and occasional credit card carryover after irregular schedules; keeps finances separate from family to avoid scrutiny over spending.
recent_transactions: Co-op maintenance fee and property tax escrow payment
Copay for nephrology appointment and lab work
Discrete hotel booking in Brooklyn for a weekend away (kept off shared family narratives)
Monthly MetroCard and occasional rideshare to JFK for early report times

relationships: Mai Nguyen (older sister in Staten Island) — close but politically and religiously conservative; Ingrid avoids personal topics
Huy Nguyen (nephew) — helps with tech, but Ingrid is cautious about what he can see on her phone
Evelyn Park (neighbor in Queens) — trusted friend who knows about Ingrid’s orientation and provides emotional support
Rosa Delgado (longtime flight attendant colleague) — confidante on work stress; not fully aware of Ingrid’s personal life
Church community in Flushing — primary social circle where Ingrid presents a carefully managed version of herself
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive: follows airline updates, travel pages, and church groups; posts occasional photos of landmarks from layovers with neutral captions; keeps friend list curated and avoids relationship indicators.
private_conversations: Discussions with Evelyn about fear of being outed at church and family gatherings
Texting with a woman she met through an LGBTQ seniors meetup, using coded language and deleting message threads
Conversations about whether to attend Pride-related events anonymously and the risk of being recognized
Carefully worded explanations to family about why she travels alone and does not date men

schedule.work_hours: Irregular rotating schedule; typically 3–4 day trips with early reports (often 5–7 a.m.) and occasional red-eyes, averaging 70–85 flight hours/month depending on bidding and health.
regular_appointments: Monthly lab work for kidney function and electrolytes
Blood pressure check-in with primary care every 3–4 months
Physical therapy once weekly when knee pain flares
Sunday morning church service (attendance is socially important)
upcoming_events: Nephrology follow-up in 6 weeks to review labs and medication plan
Family dinner in Staten Island for a cousin’s engagement (high stress due to questions about her personal life)
Two-day JFK-based domestic trip pairing next week (bidded for to avoid overnighting with a specific coworker who gossips)
LGBTQ seniors discussion group in Manhattan (attending under first-name-only)
schedule.daily_routine: On workdays she wakes early, checks bid changes, packs low-sodium snacks, and wears compression socks; on off-days she walks in Flushing Meadows-Corona Park, cooks simple kidney-friendly meals, and limits evening outings to avoid questions from neighbors/church acquaintances.

professional.company: Major U.S. legacy airline based out of JFK/LGA with international routes
projects: Annual recurrent safety and emergency procedures training
Mentoring a newly hired flight attendant on service standards and passenger de-escalation
Volunteering for the cabin safety committee briefing on hydration and fatigue management for older crew
professional.performance_issues: Occasional fatigue on multi-leg days due to insomnia and medical appointments; actively avoids discussing personal life after a coworker hinted at rumors, which makes her appear guarded during layovers.
workplace_relationships: Supportive relationship with a senior purser who accommodates medical appointment days when possible
Tension with a gossip-prone coworker who asks intrusive questions about her dating life and church
Friendly but surface-level rapport with most crew; keeps boundaries to reduce risk of being outed

hobbies: Walking in city parks and along the waterfront
Photography of architecture and street scenes
Cooking Vietnamese dishes modified for lower sodium
Library book clubs (attends selectively to avoid community overlap)
habits: Tracks blood pressure at home and logs readings
Limits sodium and avoids NSAIDs to protect kidney function
Keeps a separate, passcode-protected email for LGBTQ group communications
Deletes messages and uses neutral contact names to reduce the chance of being outed
Uses compression socks and stays hydrated during flights
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a modest co-op apartment in Queens; maintains a carefully curated, conventional image for neighbors and church contacts while keeping her private life compartmentalized.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Low-sodium, kidney-conscious eating with moderate protein; prefers home-cooked meals, avoids heavily processed foods, and limits alcohol to an occasional glass of wine on trips where she feels anonymous.
